submission.title: NIST CAISI RFI: Empirical Agent Trust Detection Primitives
submission.summary: We present 300+ detection primitives for AI agent security, backed by empirical testing across 4 live test cases (TC1-TC4) and grounded in peer-reviewed research. Our approach treats trust as measurable behavioral evidence, not declared identity.

1. Threats & Risks to AI Agent Systems.question: Q1a: prompt injection at scale
1. Threats & Risks to AI Agent Systems.description: Kaya et al (IEEE S&P 2026): 13% e-commerce exposed. 14 detection patterns.

1. Threats & Risks to AI Agent Systems.question: Q1a: correlated hallucination
1. Threats & Risks to AI Agent Systems.description: Kim et al (ICML 2025): 60% agreement when both wrong.

1. Threats & Risks to AI Agent Systems.question: Q1a: silent failure modes
1. Threats & Risks to AI Agent Systems.description: Abyrint/Strand (2025): 4 archetypes. System proceeds as if fine.

test_cases.description: First live verify-then-pay via PayLock escrow

test_cases.key_finding: clove Δ50 — social vs financial signals diverge

key_differentiators: Empirical: 300+ runnable scripts, not theoretical frameworks
Live infrastructure: isnad sandbox with Ed25519 attestation
Cross-agent validation: TC3/TC4 with independent scorers
Open: all scripts available for reproduction
